body:
## What changed

- switched frontend route pages in `CardGame/frontend/src/router/index.jsx` from eager imports to `React.lazy(...)`
- added a shared `withSuspense(...)` wrapper so routed pages render through one loading fallback
- kept the existing `RootLayout` and `PrivateRoute` behavior unchanged

## Why

The router was synchronously importing every page, which pulled all routed page code into the initial frontend bundle. This change makes page code load on demand when the route is visited.

## Impact

- reduces initial route bundle cost
- preserves current route protection and layout behavior
- keeps the change scoped to the router only

## Validation

- verified public and protected route navigation after the router change
- ran `npm run build` in `CardGame/frontend`
- build output showed route chunk splitting for pages such as `HomePage`, `LobbyPage`, `LeaderboardPage`, and `GamePage`

## Notes

- `CardGame/frontend` currently has no `lint` script, so there was no frontend lint command available to run before PR creation
- the build completes asset generation, but `vite-bundle-analyzer` exits afterward if port `8888` is already in use; this is an existing tooling/environment issue and not caused by the router change
title: feat(frontend): lazy-load route pages
